title: GrantLayer Pilot Review Agenda
description: Machine-readable agenda for a first technical pilot partner review meeting. Defines the meeting goal, prerequisites, ordered agenda items, demo commands, required artifacts, decision options, stop conditions, and explicit non-goals.
meetingGoal: Confirm partner understanding of the GrantLayer Product Core, map one real workflow to GrantLayer records, identify integration touchpoints, surface production-hardening gaps, and agree on pilot success criteria or blockers.
prerequisites: Partner can run python3 scripts/demo/gl061_api_smoke.py --dry-run locally
Partner can run python3 -m unittest discover backend.tests -v locally

agendaItems.title: Product statement and problem fit
agendaItems.goal: Confirm the partner understands the core value proposition and sees a problem fit.
agendaItems.deliverable: Partner can restate the product statement in their own words.

agendaItems.title: Partner workflow overview
agendaItems.goal: Understand the partner's current grant or funding workflow and pain points.
agendaItems.deliverable: High-level workflow diagram or description shared by the partner.

agendaItems.title: GrantLayer Product Core walkthrough
agendaItems.goal: Walk through the 12 Product Core stages from grant request to compliance readiness.
agendaItems.deliverable: Partner can name each stage and its purpose.

agendaItems.title: Demo scenario review
agendaItems.goal: Trace the municipality microgrant scenario and map it to the partner's domain.
agendaItems.deliverable: Partner identifies at least one analogous step in their own workflow.

agendaItems.title: GL-057 example JSON review
agendaItems.goal: Inspect the 12 static JSON examples and explain stable identifier relationships.
agendaItems.deliverable: Partner traces a stable ID through at least three example files.

agendaItems.title: GL-058 minimal API usage walkthrough
agendaItems.goal: Map each Product Core stage to an OpenAPI path and explain contract boundaries.
agendaItems.deliverable: Partner can name the OpenAPI path for grant request creation and grant retrieval.

agendaItems.title: GL-061 demo runner dry-run
agendaItems.goal: Run the demo runner locally to demonstrate artifact coherence.
agendaItems.deliverable: Dry-run completes with zero errors and the partner sees the ordered smoke plan.

agendaItems.title: Integration touchpoint mapping
agendaItems.goal: Identify where the partner's system would connect to GrantLayer.
agendaItems.deliverable: A list of at least two candidate integration touchpoints (e.g., intake, approval, evidence bundle, auditor export).

agendaItems.title: Non-goals and production-hardening gaps
agendaItems.goal: Explicitly surface what is not included and what production operation would require.
agendaItems.deliverable: Partner lists at least three production-hardening priorities and acknowledges non-goals.

agendaItems.title: Pilot success criteria
agendaItems.goal: Review the success criteria checklist and agree on what success means for this phase.
agendaItems.deliverable: A signed-off or agreed list of 3–5 pilot success criteria.

agendaItems.title: Next decision
agendaItems.goal: Choose one recommended next path and schedule a follow-up if needed.
agendaItems.deliverable: A single chosen decision option and a follow-up date or condition.

demoCommands.purpose: Demonstrate local artifact coherence without network access.
Validate that the pilot partner preparation pack is present and coherent.
Validate that the demo runner and smoke manifest are present and coherent.
Run the full backend suite to confirm zero failures and zero errors.

requiredArtifacts.title: Minimum Viable Integration Guide
requiredArtifacts.purpose: Explains what GrantLayer is and what it solves.

requiredArtifacts.title: Integrator Quickstart
requiredArtifacts.purpose: Minimal static JSON examples for quick integrator onboarding.

requiredArtifacts.title: Minimal API Usage Walkthrough
requiredArtifacts.purpose: Maps Product Core stages to OpenAPI paths.

requiredArtifacts.title: Integration Demo Scenario
requiredArtifacts.purpose: Concrete deterministic example with actor definitions.

requiredArtifacts.title: Pilot-Ready Handoff Plan
requiredArtifacts.purpose: Technical handoff package for pilot discussions.

requiredArtifacts.title: Pilot-Ready Release Decision
requiredArtifacts.purpose: Formal release decision recording the current Pilot-Ready state.

requiredArtifacts.title: Demo Runner / API Smoke Script
requiredArtifacts.purpose: Local dry-run confidence check for Pilot-Ready artifacts.

requiredArtifacts.title: Pilot Partner Preparation Pack
requiredArtifacts.purpose: This document — the practical package for the pilot partner discussion.

decisionOptions.label: Proceed to scoped pilot plan
decisionOptions.description: Define a time-boxed pilot with the partner, specific success criteria, and a clear endpoint.

decisionOptions.label: Run local API smoke v2
decisionOptions.description: Extend the GL-061 demo runner to exercise more edge cases or integrate a lightweight local API client.

decisionOptions.label: Prioritize production-hardening roadmap
decisionOptions.description: Rank critical gaps and plan a hardening sprint before the next partner discussion.

decisionOptions.label: Pause for API / OpenAPI contract hardening
decisionOptions.description: Invest in tightening the OpenAPI specification and contract tests before proceeding.

stopConditions: The full backend suite is failing.
Any pilot handoff artifact is missing or unreadable.
Non-goals are unclear or have been silently promoted to in-scope features.
Production readiness is being claimed accidentally or implicitly.
Credentials or real personal data appear in examples, fixtures, or documentation.
The partner expects production SaaS deployment in this phase.
A P0 or P1 blocker is identified that prevents pilot planning.
nonGoals: Production deployment (containers, load balancing, TLS termination, orchestration)
Production authentication (OAuth, JWT, SSO, HSM-backed key management)
Production key material (demo Ed25519 keypair and synthetic data only)
Production observability (metrics, logging pipelines, alerting, tracing)
Backup / restore (no automated backup, point-in-time recovery, or disaster runbooks)
SDKs (no client libraries for Python, JavaScript, or Go)
Legal or compliance certification (outputs are structured records, not legal advice)
Blockchain / wallet / payment integration (SHA-256 and Ed25519 only)
Dashboard / UI (HTML dashboard is present but not productized)
Multi-tenant SaaS (no tenant isolation, subscription billing, or SaaS onboarding)
